rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Verified Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 13, 2008 LINE 3. PETITIONERS RHONDA COLVIN AND VINCENT WEBSTER, JR.'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To File Verified Second Amended Complaint: Denied without prejudice. The original complaint asserts that decedent's overriding wish was to have the board and care operation continue and that Antwain be groomed to take over the facility.( P. 7) The complaint also asserts that decedent wanted to leave the house to her great grandchildren, including Vincent Webster, that she wanted Reverend Riddick to be the executor (P. 8, 10) and that she wanted to avoid the litigation between the grandchildren. (P. 11) On August 4, 2004 it is alleged that "Choyce purportedly produced the Will and read the Will to Decedent." At that time, decedent is alleged to have stated she did not want the house sold and wanted to avoid litigation over her estate. (P. 14) The second amended complaint alleges in paragraph 6 that "Choyce purportedly read to Decedent was that all of Decedent's property went to Toshia in trust for the benefit of Decedent's great grandchildren. What Choyce purportedly read, however, was not what was in the will." In paragraph 7 decedent is alleged to have said at this time that "I do not want a fight over this Will -- It is my intention that all my estated be distributed to Reverend Riddick to be held in trust for all my great grand children." Paragraph 27 restates the allegations of paragraph 6. The Court finds that the proposed amendment is not a sham and that Vincent Webster may have a claim against defendants. The Canatella Declaration reflects that the facts giving rise to the amendment occurred on February 8, 2008. However, the Declaration does not explain why Webster was not named in the original complaint and for that reason only, the amendment is denied without prejudice to renew the request. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: TERREE ROUSH et al VS. THE COCA-COLA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint Stating A Request For Punitive Damages Based Upon The Ssame Facts Supporting The Negligence Cause Of Action
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 13, 2008 LINE 4. PLAINTIFFS TERREE ROUSH AND RICK ROUSH'S Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint Stating A Request For Punitive Damages Based Upon The Same Facts Supporting The Negligence Cause Of Action IS DENIED. JUDGE BALLATI'S ORDER OF SEPTEMBER 21, 2007 PRECLUDES FURTHER MOTIONS AND THAT ORDER HAS NOT BEEN MODIFIED. CONTRARY TO PLAINTIFF'S CONTENTIONS, THE AMENDMENT WOULD PREJUDICE DEFENDANTS UNLESS DEFENDANTS HAD THE OPPORTUNITY TO CONDUCT DISCOVERY AND TO FILE DISPOSITIVE MOTIONS, AND BOTH ARE PRECLUDED BY THE COURT ORDER OF SEPTEMBER 21, 2007. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: EARNEST WARD VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 13, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. IT IS FAR FROM CLEAR THAT DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES FAIL TO REQUIRE THAT PLAINTIFF DESCRIBE WHAT TESTIMONY A WITNESS IS CAPABLE OF GIVING. IF THE BURDEN SHIFTED, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT THROUGH THE DECLARATIONS OF WARD AND EDWARDS. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO THESE DECLARATIONS ARE OVERRULED. =(302/PJM/VC)
